---
title: "Group8_assignment_2"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
date: "2026-07-08"
---

# (a)

```{R}
# Group 8 assignment 
# Calling my packages
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Working with the mtcars data set 
## Loading the data set 
Mtcars <- mtcars

## Displaying the first six observations 
head(Mtcars)
```

- A brief description of the structure of the mtcars data set:
  - It has the name of the car model in the first column, followed by the mpg, cyl, disp, hp, drat, wt, qsec, vs, am, gear, and carb. They are all of the dbl data type. 
  
# (b) 
## (i)
```{R}


# Performing descriptive analytics on the variable mpg 
mean(Mtcars$mpg) # mean 
sd(Mtcars$mpg) # standard deviation 
```

- Mean = 20.09062
- Standard deviation =  6.026948

## (ii)
```{R}
## Histogram for mpg 
ggplot2::ggplot(data = Mtcars, mapping = aes(x = mpg)) + 
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1.5)
```

- Most of the data is clustered between approximately 12.5 and 27.5 miles per gallon 

# (c)
```{R}
# Creating a scatter plot of mpg vs hp
ggplot2::ggplot(data = Mtcars, mapping = aes(x = hp, y = mpg)) + 
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs (
    x = "horsepower",
    y = "miles per gallon"
  )

```

- There is a linear relationship 

# (d)
```{R}
# Creating a simple linear regression model with mpg as the response variable and hp as the predictor variable 
linear_model <- lm(mpg ~ hp, data = Mtcars)
summary(linear_model) # viewing the results of the linear model
```

## (i)
- y = 30.09886 - 0.06823x

## (ii)
- The coefficient implies that for every addition unit of horsepower, the miles per gallon reduce by 0.06823

## (iii)
- The coefficient of determination = 0.6024 
    - or (60.24%)

# (e)
```{R}
# Predicting the fuel efficiency for a car with hp = 150 
predict(linear_model, data.frame(hp = c(150)))
```

- For a car with hp = 150 the linear model predicts an mpg of 19.86462
